ksa_text: Design and build computer vision systems for defined business applications, including dataset preparation, model training, evaluation, deployment, and performance monitoring.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of secure software deployment methodologies, tools, and practices.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of data visualization tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Knowledge of secure software deployment methodologies, tools, and practices.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: AI/ML Engineering falls outside the primary competency scope of the VP Data Governance. This task KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is AI/ML Engineering truly out-of-scope for the VP Data Governance? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list AI/ML Engineering as a core or recommended competency for the VP Data Governance. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms AI/ML Engineering falls outside the established competency profile for the VP Data Governance. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: AI/ML Engineering is outside the competency scope of the VP Data Governance. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
